prerequisite.comment: id of a vuln that must be exploited first (chained challenge)

chain.comment: kill-chain id this vuln is a link of (Phase C); links share a chain id and an end-to-end chain test proves the full path

defense.comment: a real defensive layer the exploit must DEFEAT (Phase C), e.g. keyword-waf - the naive payload is blocked; only a bypass fires

decoy.comment: true = a look-vulnerable-but-correctly-secured endpoint (Phase C C4 red herring); intentional:false, has a holds-test proving the bait attack fails

gating.comment: how the vulnerable-vs-secure behavior is selected on the SAME route (e.g. VF_HARDENED, or a Phase-C state condition) - replaces -vuln/-SAFE route tells

allOf.comment: Intentional vulns must declare the detector that should catch them and a repro.
